# Project Constitution

<!-- Generated by `spec-kitty constitution generate` — last updated manually -->

Generated: 2026-04-30

---

## Goal — Creative

Fogreach is a browser-based action dungeon crawler (Phaser 3) in which the player, as a former Archivsmith, uncovers an occult conspiracy. The goal is to restore collective memory and decentralize knowledge before the city is consumed by darkness.

**Tags**: combat-driven, lore-rich, gothic atmosphere, 60fps desktop

---

## Goal — Commercial

### Replayability & Motivation Spiral
- Maximum replayability through procedurally generated dungeons, seasonal mechanics, and endgame loops
- Motivation spiral: every session delivers tangible progress (loot, lore, faction influence) — players return for "just one more run"
- Seasonal content: rotating modifiers, temporary bosses, leaderboards, season-exclusive items

### Political Message (Donor Strategy)
- Core thesis: authoritarian systems disguise themselves as order and democracy — and function only through the obedience, fear, and habit of citizens
- The fog as a metaphor for collective amnesia, media control, and knowledge suppression
- Light, writing, and decentralization as counterforces — directly compatible with narratives around free speech, access to knowledge, and civic resistance
- Positioning toward political think tanks, freedom initiatives, and donors: Fogreach communicates these values through gameplay — broadly accessible and low-threshold

---

## Storyline

### Setting
Fogreach — a dark medieval city shrouded in permanent mist. The Chain Council (Kettenrat) governs as a sham democracy whose factions compete outwardly but serve a unified occult agenda. The fog is not weather: it erases memories, conceals truth, and eliminates individuality.

### Protagonist
A former Archivsmith — craftsman-scholar who maintained the city's mechanical archive. After an accident at the Archive Forge, he loses parts of his memory. Noticing contradictions in official history, he begins uncovering the council's deception. His goal: decentralize knowledge and restore collective memory.

### Narrative Arc

**Act 1 — Awakening**
Tutorial and mechanics introduction. The fog feels unnatural. First council mission: the mayor's missing daughter. Underground hideouts, first faction contacts. Player learns the hub world.

**Act 2 — Obedience vs. Memory**
Council missions (document retrieval, surveillance, espionage) contrast with private jobs and forbidden lore fragments. First hints of Chain Council rituals surface. Player must decide how much to comply.

**Act 3 — Descent**
The catacombs open. Demonic pacts are revealed as the foundation of the council's power. Faction decision point: cooperate with the council or oppose it? Deeper dungeons, stronger enemies.

**Act 4 — Rebellion**
Archive Forge and Printing House reclaimed as resistance tools. Edicts published that alter city-wide mechanics. Allies won or lost based on prior decisions.

**Act 5 — Revelation**
The council's final illusion collapses. Final confrontation. Multiple endings depending on faction standing and accumulated choices.

### Quest Types
- **Official missions** — council orders, document retrieval, patrol assignments
- **Private missions** — from citizens, covert, not council-sanctioned
- **Espionage** — undercover work, information gathering, identity switching
- **Random events** — emergent occurrences in the hub world and dungeons

---

## Gameplay

### Hub World
| Location | Function | Gameplay |
|---|---|---|
| **Archive Forge** | Memory preservation workshop, partly corrupted | Crafting, upgrading, decoding lost inscriptions |
| **Town Hall & Catacombs (Rathauskeller)** | Council HQ above; forbidden rituals below | Dungeon runs, combat, lore fragments, boss encounters |
| **Printing House** | Propaganda center of the city | Story progression, faction influence, unlocking edicts |

### Combat
- WASD + mouse aim (ARPG scheme) or Arrows+QWER (classic) — both coexist, no keyboard ghosting
- Enemy variants: Grunts, Archers, Spellcasters, Boss variants with unique mechanics
- Status effects, Fog-of-War, procedural rooms, door system, spawn control

### Factions
Multiple factions with distinct agendas. Player can align, play them off each other, or go independent. Secret organizations may support or obstruct. Political intrigue is layered throughout.

---

## Pacing

### Early Game (Acts 1–2) — min. 6 hours
Linear story focus, learning mechanics, building out the hub world. All three hub locations introduced. Dungeons are short and readable.

**Dungeon session**: 10–20 min per run. Player returns to hub after each completed room or objective — sell loot, start crafting, collect quest progress.
**Hub visit**: 3–5 min.

### Mid Game (Acts 3–4) — min. 8 hours
Open exploration, deeper catacombs, faction decisions, stronger itemization. Dungeons become longer and more branched.

**Dungeon session**: 20–40 min. Hub visits become more strategic — optimize loadout before the run, plan crafting.
**Hub visit**: 5–10 min.

### Endgame — open-ended (Vampire Survivors loop)
Endless wave mode, build optimization, season runs. Shorter but denser runs, similar to Baal runs in Diablo 2.

**Dungeon session**: 15–25 min per run.
**Hub visit**: 2–3 min for quick reforging and loadout swap.

### Seasonal Mechanics
Rotating modifiers change dungeon rules temporarily (e.g. enhanced fog effects, special loot tables, boss variants). Leaderboards and season-exclusive items drive return visits.

---

## Metaprogression & Itemization

### Equipment Slots
4 slots per character: Weapon / Head / Body / Feet

| Slot | Available Items |
|---|---|
| Weapon | 4 melee (Iron Blade, Shadow Dagger, Ember Axe, Ember Bow) + 3 bows |
| Head | Chain Coif, Bronze Helm, Serpent Mask |
| Body | Leather Hauberk, Plate Armor, Shadow Coat |
| Feet | Leather Boots, Steel Soles, Windrunner |

### Consumables & Currency
- **Healing Potions**: 4 tiers — Minor (30% HP) / Normal (60%) / Major (100%) / Super
- **Portal Scrolls**: instant town teleport
- **Iron Chunks**: crafting material
- **Gold**: vendor currency

### Item Tiers (Affix-based)
| Tier | Name | Affixes | Drop Rate | Color |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Common | 0 | ~78% | Grey |
| 1 | Magic | 1 | ~20% | Blue |
| 2 | Rare | 2 | ~2% | Gold |
| 3 | Legendary | 3 | ~0.3% | Orange |

Every item has an **iLevel** (set by the enemy/chest that dropped it — determines which affixes can roll) and a **requiredLevel** to equip.

### Affix System (24 global definitions)
- **Base (6)**: Damage, Armor, HP, Speed, Crit, Range
- **Defensive (4)**: Fire/Frost/Lightning Resistance, Life Steal
- **Per-Ability (10)**: 5x Damage + 5x Cooldown for Whirl / Charged / Storm Strike / Dagger Throw / Shield Bash
- **Global (2, from iLvl 8+)**: All-Skill-Damage, All-Cooldown
- **Luxury (2)**: XP Bonus, Gold Find

### Loot Sources
- **Enemies**: Gold always drops; 2% item chance + Elite bonus (Elite +1 tier, Mini-Boss +2 tiers)
- **Chests**: tier scales with dungeon depth
- **Vendor (Mara)**: 6–8 pre-rolled items, refreshes each run
- **Quest items**: 10% drop chance while quest is active

### Elite Enemies as Loot Driver
- **Champion (Wave 6+)**: 1 affix, +50% HP, tint + glow effect
- **Unique (Wave 16+)**: 2–3 affixes, +100% HP, guaranteed Magic+ drop, named tag (e.g. "Fanatic Lightning Enchanted Brute")
- ~10 elite affixes: Fanatic, Lightning Enchanted, Cold Aura, Vampiric, Multishot, Berserker, and others

### Archive Forge (Crafting)
- **Enhance**: Iron Chunks → upgrade item tier (Common → Legendary)
- **Salvage**: break down item → Iron Chunks
- **Reroll (Mara Black Market)**: Item + Gold → re-roll affixes at same tier. Formula: base × tier-multiplier × iLvl-multiplier
- Crafting deliberately kept flat (Enhance / Salvage only) — complexity lives in the affix rolls, not the crafting tree

### Inventory
- 20 free slots (5×4 grid) + 4 equipment slots
- Stacking: Potions and materials stack up to 5; equipment does not stack
- Auto-pickup for Gold, Quest items, and drops on overlap

### Vendor (Mara)
- Minor Potion: 25G / Normal: 75G / Major: 200G / Super: 500G
- Items: dynamically priced by tier + iLvl

### Permanent Progression
- **Faction influence**: permanently unlocks merchants, mission types, and story branches
- **Printing House edicts**: permanently alter city-wide mechanics (patrol routes, prices, area access)
- **Knowledge tree**: invest lore fragments for passive bonuses and new abilities
- **Narrative gating**: main story progress unlocks new hub areas and dungeon depths

---

## Technical Standards

### Stack
JavaScript (ES6+), Phaser 3, Tiled tilemaps, HTML5 Canvas. No build pipeline. Desktop focus (Edge, Chrome, Firefox).

### Testing
No formal test suite. Manual playtesting is primary. For risky logic changes (combat, AI state machines, collision), verify manually before committing.

### Quality Gates
Manual playtest passed, no console errors, combat and enemy AI behave correctly, no regressions in existing scenes.

### Performance
60fps on desktop browsers. Scene transitions under 1 second. No memory leaks in long play sessions.

### Deployment
Browser-based, static deployment. Live: https://baechtoldleroy.github.io/Fogreach/. No server required.

---

## Coding Guidelines
- Phaser best practices; keep scenes modular
- Tilemap levels: keep map data separate from game logic
- Enemy AI: state-machine pattern preferred
- No premature abstraction — solve the immediate problem cleanly
- Comment intent, not mechanics

---

## Spec Kitty Governance

### Branch Strategy
No review required. Merge when playtest passes and no console errors.

### Governance Config
```yaml
mission: software-dev
selected_paradigms: [clean-code, minimal-tooling, modular-scenes]
selected_directives: [CLEAN_CODE, MINIMAL_TOOLING, MODULAR_SCENES]
available_tools: [git, spec-kitty, node, npm, phaser]
template_set: software-dev-default
```

### Reference Index
| Reference ID | Kind | Summary |
|---|---|---|
| `USER:PROJECT_PROFILE` | user_profile | Project-specific interview answers captured for constitution compilation |
| `PARADIGM:clean-code` | paradigm | Prefer readable, minimal, and intentional code over clever abstractions |
| `DIRECTIVE:MINIMAL_TOOLING` | directive | Keep toolchain lean and repo-local; avoid build complexity |
| `TEMPLATE_SET:software-dev-default` | template_set | Build high-quality software with structured workflows |

### Amendment Process
Amendments are proposed by PR and reviewed before adoption.

### Exception Policy
Exceptions must include rationale and expiration criteria.
